result.summary: RudderStack exposes documented HTTP APIs (including HTTP API, Test API, and Activation API), a Rudder CLI, and a RudderStack MCP server; the documented auth surface centers on RudderStack access tokens, with Basic auth documented for the HTTP and Test APIs and token-based auth for the CLI/MCP.
result.description: RudderStack is a customer data platform for collecting, transforming, governing, and routing event data, plus warehouse activation and profile capabilities. It provides infrastructure for moving customer data between apps, warehouses, and downstream tools.

rudder_access_token.label: RudderStack access token (Personal Access Token or Service Access Token)
rudder_access_token.setup: For automation, create a Service Access Token in the RudderStack dashboard from the access-management settings; RudderStack recommends SATs for production use. For user-scoped access, you can instead create a Personal Access Token from [Personal Access Tokens](https://www.rudderstack.com/docs/access-management/personal-access-tokens). See [Service Access Tokens](https://www.rudderstack.com/docs/access-management/service-access-tokens) and [Access Management Glossary](https://www.rudderstack.com/docs/access-management/glossary/) for the PAT vs SAT distinction.

surfaces.name: Rudder CLI
surfaces.notes: RudderStack documentation says the CLI authenticates with an access token and recommends a workspace-level Service Access Token for production use; Personal Access Tokens are also supported in some walkthroughs.
